marketFocus: Operation Epic Fury Day 3: Khamenei confirmed dead, Strait of Hormuz closed by IRGC, Iran retaliates against US/UK/Gulf assets — Brent crude surged 10%+ at open before paring to sub-$78/bbl
DXY firmer on haven demand, EUR/USD gapped below 1.1800, GBP/USD fell on Iran targeting UK Cyprus base — SNB signals readiness to intervene in FX market
APAC stocks mostly pressured: Nikkei 225 fell below 58,000, ASX 200 rangebound (energy up, airlines/tech down), Hang Seng mixed — BoJ Himino reaffirms rate hike path if outlook met

USD.name: USD
USD.summary: DXY firmer as Operation Epic Fury drives a sharp rotation into the Greenback as the world's premier liquidity haven. Gains are capped as cyclical peers gradually claw back some lost ground, but the structural bid remains intact. HSBC notes USD likely holds upper hand near-term, contrasting with June 2025 when initial strength was short-lived due to US policy uncertainty. ISM Manufacturing PMI due today is the first key data test of the week.
drivers: Operation Epic Fury: Strait of Hormuz closed, 27 US bases targeted — liquidity haven bid
DXY firmer on downbeat risk tone, gains capped as peers gradually recover
HSBC: USD likely holds upper hand near-term vs June 2025 pattern
Goldman Sachs: $18/bbl real-time risk premium in crude oil prices
Today: ISM Manufacturing PMI (Feb) — key data test

EUR.name: EUR
EUR.summary: EUR/USD gapped below the 1.1800 handle at the open as the Iran conflict threatens global disruption and energy supply chains. The Eurozone is a major net energy importer, making it acutely exposed to Strait of Hormuz disruption. German Chancellor Merz voiced concern over the fallout ahead of his Washington trip Tuesday to seek clarity on tariffs. ECB's Nagel said doubts about USD safe-haven status have increased, but EUR is not benefiting from this rotation.
drivers: EUR/USD gapped below 1.1800 — Eurozone is major net energy importer, exposed to Hormuz
German Chancellor Merz meets Trump in Washington Tuesday to seek tariff clarity
ECB's Nagel: doubts about USD safe-haven status increased, dollar weakness to remain
European equity futures down 1.5% at open (Euro Stoxx 50)
Tuesday: Core CPI Flash Estimate y/y (11:00 Frankfurt Time) — key data

GBP.name: GBP
GBP.summary: GBP/USD fell at the open amid pressure in cyclicals and with Iran targeting a UK military base in Cyprus. UK PM Starmer confirmed British planes are 'in the sky' in coordinated defence against Iran, and the UK was reportedly drawing up options for a mass evacuation of citizens from the Middle East. The MFS credit collapse from last week continues to weigh on UK financial sector sentiment, adding to the bearish confluence.
drivers: Iran targeted UK RAF base in Cyprus — GBP sold on geopolitical risk
UK PM Starmer: British planes 'in the sky' defending against Iran
UK reportedly drawing up mass evacuation plans for Middle East citizens
MFS credit collapse (fraud) continues to weigh on UK financials
UK RAF jets took out Iranian drone heading to Qatar (UK directly involved)

JPY.name: JPY
JPY.summary: USD/JPY choppy with early haven demand supporting both the Greenback and the Yen simultaneously. BoJ Deputy Governor Himino reaffirmed the rate hike path: 'raise rates if economic outlook is met, goal is to maintain price stability.' The Yen benefits from carry unwind as risk-off conditions force unwinding of JPY-funded carry trades. Nikkei 225 fell below 58,000 as exporters suffered from the worsening geopolitical climate.
drivers: BoJ Deputy Governor Himino: 'raise rates if economic outlook is met'
Carry unwind: risk-off forces unwinding of JPY-funded carry trades
Nikkei 225 fell below 58,000 on geopolitical pressure and Hormuz disruption
USD/JPY choppy — dual haven demand (USD + JPY) creates competition
Japanese S&P Global Manufacturing PMI Final (Feb) 53.0 (exp 52.8) — beat

AUD.name: AUD
AUD.summary: AUD slumped at the open as risk-off conditions hit high-beta currencies hardest. ASX 200 was rangebound with weakness in tech, financials, and airlines (double whammy: flight disruptions + higher fuel costs), while energy stocks surged on oil. Qantas down more than 10% on travel disruptions. AUD gradually recovered from initial extreme lows as moves moderated, but the bearish bias remains intact given China exposure and risk-off environment.
drivers: AUD slumped at open on risk-off, high-beta status exposed
ASX 200 rangebound: airlines -10%+ (Qantas), energy +11%+ (Woodside)
Strait of Hormuz closure threatens China supply chains — AUD China proxy
Australian S&P Global Manufacturing PMI Final (Feb) 51.0 (prelim 51.5) — slight miss
Antipodeans gradually recovered from extreme lows as moves moderated

CAD.name: CAD
CAD.summary: CAD faces a conflicting setup: the energy windfall from surging crude prices (Brent +10% at open) provides a tailwind, but domestic weakness and the broader risk-off environment cap gains. Canada is a net energy exporter, so Hormuz disruption and higher oil prices are structurally positive for CAD, but the risk-off rotation into USD and JPY limits the upside. BoC Governor Macklem speaks Wednesday.
drivers: Crude oil surge (Brent +10% at open) — CAD benefits as net energy exporter
Risk-off rotation into USD/JPY caps CAD upside despite energy windfall
OPEC+ agreed to raise output by 206k BPD in April (exp 137k) — above expectations
BoC Governor Macklem speaks Wednesday — key for CAD direction
US-Canada trade uncertainty persists (USMCA, tariff overhang)

CHF.name: CHF
CHF.summary: CHF benefits from strong haven demand as Operation Epic Fury drives capital preservation flows into the Swiss Franc. However, the SNB signalled it is 'more prepared to intervene in the FX market' given the international situation, which caps CHF appreciation. The SNB's intervention threat creates a ceiling on CHF strength but does not reverse the underlying haven bid. CHF remains structurally bullish in risk-off environments.
drivers: Haven demand drives CHF higher on Operation Epic Fury risk-off
SNB: 'more prepared to intervene in FX market' given international situation — caps upside
Swiss Retail Sales MoM (Jan) 1.1% (prev 1.0%) — mild positive
Swiss Retail Sales YoY (Jan) -1.1% (exp 2.7%) — miss, but geopolitics dominate
EUR/CHF under pressure as EUR weakens and CHF benefits from haven flows

NZD.name: NZD
NZD.summary: NZD slumped at the open alongside AUD as risk-off conditions hit high-beta currencies hardest. Antipodeans gradually recovered from initial extreme lows as moves moderated, but the bearish bias remains intact. NZD is the most vulnerable G10 currency in risk-off environments due to its high-beta nature, RBNZ dovish stance, and China trade exposure. The Strait of Hormuz closure threatens China supply chains, which weighs on NZD as a China proxy.
drivers: NZD slumped at open on risk-off, most vulnerable G10 high-beta currency
Strait of Hormuz closure threatens China supply chains — NZD China proxy
RBNZ dovish stance provides no fundamental support
Antipodeans gradually recovered from extreme lows as moves moderated
Risk-off rotation into USD/JPY/CHF dominates the session

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: 10yr UST futures saw early haven flows but failed to sustain gains and returned to relatively flat territory as higher oil prices increase inflationary pressures. The conflicting forces — haven demand (bullish for bonds) vs. oil-driven inflation fears (bearish for bonds) — create a tug-of-war. Near-term, haven demand likely dominates in the early session, but the inflation narrative could reassert as the week progresses.
drivers: Early haven flows into USTs on Operation Epic Fury risk-off
Higher oil prices increase inflationary pressures — caps bond gains
Fed rate cut bets eased for 2026 amid credit concerns and inflation risk
T-note futures failed to sustain early gains, returned to flat
ISM Manufacturing PMI today + NFP Friday are key tests

bonds.name: German 10Y Bund
bonds.summary: Bund futures faded the majority of initial gains after printing a fresh YTD peak, but with the pullback limited. The Eurozone energy shock from Hormuz disruption supports Bund demand as a haven within Europe, but the inflationary pressure from higher oil prices creates a ceiling. German Chancellor Merz meets Trump in Washington Tuesday — tariff clarity could be a catalyst.
drivers: Bund futures printed fresh YTD peak before fading initial gains
Eurozone energy shock supports Bund demand as European haven
Higher oil prices create inflationary ceiling for Bund gains
German Chancellor Merz meets Trump Tuesday — tariff clarity catalyst
Tuesday: Eurozone CPI Flash Estimate — key for ECB rate path

bonds.name: Japan 10Y JGB
bonds.summary: 10yr JGB futures were lifted but are off initial highs as asset classes pared some of the extreme moves seen at the open. BoJ Deputy Governor Himino reaffirmed the rate hike path, which structurally pressures JGBs, but the immediate risk-off haven demand for JPY and JGBs dominates the near-term narrative.
drivers: JGB futures lifted on risk-off haven demand, off initial highs
BoJ Himino: 'raise rates if economic outlook is met' — structurally bearish for JGBs
Risk-off haven demand for JPY/JGBs dominates near-term
Nikkei 225 fell below 58,000 on geopolitical pressure
Japanese Manufacturing PMI Final (Feb) 53.0 (exp 52.8) — beat

bonds.name: UK 10Y Gilt
bonds.summary: Gilts under pressure as UK is directly involved in the Iran conflict (Cyprus base targeted, RAF jets deployed). The MFS credit collapse from last week continues to weigh on UK financial sector sentiment. UK PM Starmer's political position weakened by Green Party by-election win and the cost of military involvement in the Middle East.
drivers: Iran targeted UK RAF base in Cyprus — UK directly involved in conflict
UK RAF jets deployed in coordinated defence against Iran
MFS credit collapse continues to weigh on UK financial sector
Green Party by-election win weakens PM Starmer's political position
UK reportedly drawing up mass evacuation plans for Middle East citizens
